claims_verified.claim: Judged sha == merged sha, no re-resolution (TOCTOU pin) in --local
claims_verified.evidence: local_merge.py:648 _local_worktree resolves ref->sha then _worktree_at_sha; gather_gates:678 verified_sha=_worktree_sha(wt); merge_branch(fetch=False) merges that sha. test_local_merge.py:906 asserts gates.head_sha==sha and merge lands it

claims_verified.claim: No fetch in --local gate or merge
claims_verified.evidence: _local_worktree omits the fetch that _branch_worktree does (local_merge.py:611 vs :648); merge_branch:760 `if fetch:` guards the fetch; main wires fetch=local_ref is None (:811)
local_merge.py:796 list_ready returns args.task when local_ref set; test_cli_local_judges_local_sha_bypasses_discovery stubs discover_ready to raise, rc==0

claims_verified.claim: Full gate identical; red suite / security blocker => hold, not merge
claims_verified.evidence: gather_gates only branches on worktree source; test_local_gather_red_tests_holds & test_local_gather_security_blocker_holds assert decision=='hold'

claims_verified.claim: Dirty-tree guard refuses before any gate
claims_verified.evidence: local_merge.py:743-749 checks git status --porcelain, returns 1; test_cli_local_dirty_tree_refuses_before_any_gate stubs gather_gates to raise and asserts rc!=0, 'commit or stash' printed, nothing merged
local_merge.py:729-732 parser.error on task!=1 or blank ref; test_cli_local_arg_rules covers 0/2 tasks, blank, and no-value cases (SystemExit)

local_merge.py:764-785 branches: --local fetch check=False (warn on failure, abort 2 if reachable+diverged); else-branch keeps check=True abort. Three tests plus existing test_main_aborts_whole_run_on_tripwire

claims_verified.claim: Default path unchanged; existing tests green
claims_verified.evidence: git numstat tests/test_local_merge.py = 323 additions / 0 deletions; verify_one passes local_ref kwarg only when set; full suite 713 passed

claims_verified.claim: Digest adds local route to generic branch only
claims_verified.evidence: local_merge.py:179-185 additions in the else block; test_local_broken_digest_points_at_the_local_route and test_local_route_absent_from_infra_override_digest both green

claims_verified.claim: Launcher forwards --local and softens die to warn only under --local
claims_verified.evidence: merge-verified.sh:826-846; test_merge_verified_launcher.py both tests pass (warn+forward with --local; die without)

claims_verified.claim: No push / no hub write on --local path; fix-free preserved (engine only sources commit from a local ref)
claims_verified.evidence: no push added in local path; test asserts pushed==[] and origin bare never written; no edit/patch/cherry-pick code added

claims_verified.claim: Suite green, 0 type errors, ASCII preserved
claims_verified.evidence: ruff 'All checks passed'; basedpyright '0 errors, 70 warnings'; pytest '713 passed'; added lines contain zero non-ASCII (pre-existing NÁLEZ/§ only)

findings.summary: Launcher detects only the space form `--local`; the argparse-accepted equals form `--local=<ref>` would not soften the missing-remote die (fails closed/loud, so the mode simply won't start in a no-hub setup). The documented recipe uses the space form, so AC#10 is met; worth matching `--local=*` too for robustness.

test_assessment: Every acceptance criterion has a covering test I ran green. AC#1 test_local_gather_and_merge_uses_local_sha_no_fetch_no_push + test_cli_local_judges_local_sha_bypasses_discovery; AC#2 same (merge lands judged sha); AC#3 discover_ready-raises stub; AC#4 test_local_gather_red_tests_holds + test_local_gather_security_blocker_holds; AC#5 test_cli_local_dirty_tree_refuses_before_any_gate; AC#6 test_cli_local_arg_rules (4 cases); AC#7 three separate tests + existing default-path tripwire test; AC#8 existing file 323/0 additions and full 713-test suite green; AC#9 two digest tests (generic yes, infra-override no); AC#10 two launcher subprocess tests; AC#11 push-absent/origin-untouched assertions; AC#12 verified independently. New functions have failure-mode tests: _resolve_local_ref has test_resolve_local_ref_rejects_an_unresolvable_ref (clean raise); dirty-tree, arg-rules, and tripwire failure branches all exercised.
residual_risks: Untested branch: a worktree-path ref whose HEAD sha is absent from the target object store falls through to a clean RuntimeError (fails closed, nothing merged) - safe but uncovered.
Path(ref).is_dir() in _resolve_local_ref resolves relative to CWD, not repo; the documented recipe runs from the repo dir so it is fine, but an unusual CWD would fail to resolve (again fails closed).
Blast radius is the merge authority itself (L3); mitigated by the full default-path suite passing (713) proving no regression.
